key_requirements: Use system images WITHOUT 'Google Play' or 'google_apis_playstore' label - these are production builds that cannot run adb root
Choose either 'default' (AOSP) or 'google_apis' system images - both support adb root natively
Install system image using: sdkmanager --install 'system-images;android-36;google_apis;x86_64' or 'system-images;android-36;default;x86_64'
Create AVD using: avdmanager create avd --name <name> --package 'system-images;android-36;google_apis;x86_64'
After creating AVD, test root access with: adb root (should show 'restarting adbd as root', not 'cannot run as root in production builds')
result.system_image_type: google_apis (recommended) or default/AOSP. Both support adb root. Avoid google_apis_playstore as it uses production/user builds that block root access.
result.abi_recommendation: x86_64 for Intel/AMD systems, arm64-v8a for Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3) Macs. x86_64 typically has better performance on Intel-based systems.
critical_notes: System images labeled 'Google Play' or 'google_apis_playstore' are signed with release keys and use production/user builds - these CANNOT run adb root
The error 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds' means you're using a google_apis_playstore image - you must use google_apis or default instead
google_apis images include Google Play Services (Maps, Firebase, etc.) but NOT the Play Store app - sufficient for most development
default/AOSP images are lightweight with no Google services - use when you don't need Google APIs
For SDK 36, 'default' system images may be missing (reported issue). Use 'google_apis' as the reliable rootable option.
Build types: userdebug (rootable) vs user/production (not rootable). google_apis and default are userdebug builds.

result.root_verification_output: adbd cannot run as root in production builds
result.failure_reason: The AVD 'rootable_sdk36_x86_64' is a production build (ro.build.type=user, ro.debuggable=0). The adb root command failed with 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'. The shell user has uid=2000(shell) instead of uid=0(root). The su binary is not available. This AVD was created with a non-rooted system image despite its name suggesting it should be rootable.
